task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal application is open with 'colorado_foothills.top' loaded, visible in the title bar, file explorer dialog, or recent files.

task_completion.description: Access the slope analysis tool in TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or menu for slope analysis (likely labeled 'Pendientes' or 'Taludes' in Spanish) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Input the slope percentage thresholds: 15%, 30%, and 50%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slope analysis dialog shows input fields populated with the threshold values 15, 30, and 50 (or ranges 0-15, 15-30, 30-50, >50).

task_completion.description: Generate and display the slope analysis map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main TopoCal viewport displays a color-coded slope map or triangulated irregular network (TIN) showing the different slope zones.

task_completion.description: Create a slope report at 'slope_report.txt' including area, triangle counts, and min/max/avg slope values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or TopoCal report window showing area, triangle counts, and overall minimum, maximum, and average slope values, being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\slope_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Save the project with the slope analysis visible to 'slope_analysis.top'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog saving the file as 'slope_analysis.top' in the Documents folder, or the TopoCal title bar updating to show 'slope_analysis.top'.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through TopoCal menus and dialogs rather than using a terminal or Python script to mock the output files.

integrity.description: The slope report contains genuine calculated data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains specific, non-placeholder numerical values for areas, triangle counts, and min/max/avg slopes that appear to be generated by the software.

integrity.description: Files were saved to the exact requested directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: Save dialogs or file explorer windows confirm the files are placed exactly in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' and not just a generic or default folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The TopoCal application UI is in Spanish (look for terms like 'Pendientes/Taludes' for slope analysis). The slope thresholds to be entered are exactly 15, 30, and 50. The output project must be saved as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\slope_analysis.top' and the report as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\slope_report.txt'.
